title: podman container runlabel documentation references nonexistent --set flag
body:
<!--
---------------------------------------------------
BUG REPORT INFORMATION
---------------------------------------------------
Use the commands below to provide key information from your environment:
You do NOT have to include this information if this is a FEATURE REQUEST

**NOTE** A large number of issues reported against Podman are often found to already be fixed
in more current versions of the project.  Before reporting an issue, please verify the
version you are running with `podman version` and compare it to the latest release
documented on the top of Podman's [README.md](../README.md).  If they differ, please
update your version of Podman to the latest possible and retry your command before creating
an issue.

If you are filing a bug against `podman build`, please instead file a bug
against Buildah (https://github.com/containers/buildah/issues). Podman build
executes Buildah to perform container builds, and as such the Buildah
maintainers are best equipped to handle these bugs.
-->

**Is this a BUG REPORT or FEATURE REQUEST? (leave only one on its own line)**

/kind bug

**Description**

The podman documentation at `docs/source/markdown/podman-container-runlabel.1.md` describes a `--set NAME=VALUE` option which (as far as I can tell from looking through the git history) has never existed in the `podman container runlabel` command.  As such, it seems like the feature should either be added or the documentation updated to remove references to the missing option.

**Steps to reproduce the issue:**

1. Execute `man podman-container-runlabel` and note the following section:

```
       --set=NAME=VALUE

       Set name  value
```
2. Attempt to use the `--set` option and note that it doesn't work

```
$ podman container runlabel --display --set FOO=BAR run exampleimage
Error: unknown flag: --set
```

**Describe the results you received:**
No `--set` option available, despite documentation suggesting as much in `docs/source/markdown/podman-container-runlabel.1.md`.

**Describe the results you expected:**
Either for the `--set` option to work or the documentation to reflect its absence.

**Additional information you deem important (e.g. issue happens only occasionally):**

**Output of `podman version`:**

```
$ podman version
Version:            1.8.0
RemoteAPI Version:  1
Go Version:         go1.11.6
OS/Arch:            linux/amd64

```

**Output of `podman info --debug`:**

```
$ podman info --debug

host:

  Distribution:
    distribution: debian
    version: "10"
  IDMappings:
    gidmap:
    - container_id: 0
      host_id: 1003
      size: 1
    - container_id: 1

      size: 65536
    uidmap:

    - container_id: 1
      host_id: 296608
      size: 65536
  MemFree: 2856894464

  OCIRuntime:
    name: runc
    package: 'containerd.io: /usr/bin/runc'

    version: |-
      runc version 1.0.0-rc10
      commit: dc9208a3303feef5b3839f4323d9beb36df0a9dd
      spec: 1.0.1-dev
  SwapFree: 34025750528
  SwapTotal: 34040967168
  arch: amd64
  cpus: 8
  eventlogger: file
  hostname: search2
  kernel: 4.19.0-8-amd64
  os: linux
  rootless: true
  slirp4netns:
    Executable: /usr/bin/slirp4netns
    Package: 'slirp4netns: /usr/bin/slirp4netns'   
    Version: |-
      slirp4netns version 0.4.3
      commit: unknown
  uptime: 90h 20m 23.08s (Approximately 3.75 days) 
registries:
  search:
  - docker.io
  - quay.io
store:
  ConfigFile: /home/elastalert/.config/containers/storage.conf
  ContainerStore:
    number: 1
  GraphDriverName: overlay
  GraphOptions:
    overlay.mount_program:
      Executable: /usr/bin/fuse-overlayfs
      Package: 'fuse-overlayfs: /usr/bin/fuse-overlayfs'
      Version: |-
        fusermount3 version: 3.4.1
        fuse-overlayfs: version 0.7.6
        FUSE library version 3.4.1
        using FUSE kernel interface version 7.27   
  GraphRoot: /home/elastalert/.local/share/containers/storage
  GraphStatus:
    Backing Filesystem: extfs
    Native Overlay Diff: "false"
    Supports d_type: "true"
    Using metacopy: "false"
  ImageStore:
    number: 122
  RunRoot: /var/tmp/run-1003/containers
  VolumePath: /home/elastalert/.local/share/containers/storage/volumes
```

**Package info (e.g. output of `rpm -q podman` or `apt list podman`):**

```
$ apt list podman
Listing... Done
podman/unknown,now 1.8.0~7 amd64 [installed]
podman/unknown 1.8.0~7 arm64
podman/unknown 1.8.0~7 armhf
podman/unknown 1.8.0~7 ppc64el
```
(I have the Kubic project .debs for Debian 10 installed, as per https://podman.io/getting-started/installation.html)

**Additional environment details (AWS, VirtualBox, physical, etc.):**
This is running on a physical box.

comments.body: I've created a pull request to remove `--set` from the documentation at #5437.
@mheon @rhatdan Can we close this since the #5437 is merged? Thanks.
